seoTitle: How to Create a View in Airtable, Add Personal Views
metaDescription: Create new Airtable views in Grid, Kanban, Gallery, or Calendar layouts, and set up private personal views without disrupting your team.
intro: Airtable views let you look at the same underlying data in different ways, whether that's a Kanban board for a project or a private filtered grid just for you. This guide covers creating new views, choosing a layout type, and setting a view to personal so your filters don't affect collaborators.

steps.heading: Open the views sidebar
steps.body: Go to the sidebar in your base where existing views are listed.

steps.heading: Create a new view
steps.body: Click the plus icon to create a view and choose your preferred layout, such as Grid, Kanban, Gallery, or Calendar.

steps.heading: Name the view and set permissions
steps.body: Give the view a clear name and choose whether it's Personal, visible only to you, or Collaborative, visible to your whole team.

steps.heading: Apply filters, sorts, and grouping
steps.body: Configure filters, sort order, and grouping rules so the view surfaces exactly the records you care about.

steps.heading: Try alternative layouts for different needs
steps.body: Create additional views like Kanban or Gallery when a different visualization better suits a specific workflow or audience.

steps.heading: Organize views into sections
steps.body: Use view sections in the sidebar to keep multiple views organized and easy to navigate as your base grows.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Personal and Collaborative view?
faq.answer: A Personal view's filters and settings are visible only to you, while a Collaborative view's changes affect what everyone with access sees.

faq.question: How many views can I create in one Airtable base?
faq.answer: You can create multiple views per table, limited mainly by your plan's overall view limits rather than a small fixed number.

faq.question: Can I switch a view from Personal to Collaborative later?
faq.answer: Yes, view permissions can generally be changed after creation from the view's settings.

faq.question: Do views change the underlying data?
faq.answer: No, views only change how data is filtered, sorted, and displayed — the underlying records stay the same across all views.
